title: Nageia cuspidata
taxonomy: Nageia cuspidata is classified as a species within the genus Nageia, which is a part of the family Podocarpaceae. This family is a group of coniferous trees commonly known as the yellowwoods. The genus Nageia is native to tropical Asia and the Pacific region, and Nageia cuspidata is specifically found in Malaysia. Nageia cuspidata is a type of evergreen tree that is often used in landscaping due to its attractive foliage and growth habit. The classification of Nageia cuspidata is further supported by its shared characteristics with other members of the Podocarpaceae family.
uses: Nageia cuspidata has been traditionally used in Southeast Asia for its medicinal properties. The plant's extracts have been reported to exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities. In traditional medicine, it is used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant's leaves and bark are often used to make infusions or decoctions for medicinal purposes. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and potential side effects of using Nageia cuspidata as a medicinal plant.

intro: Nageia cuspidata, also known as the yellow nutmeg, is a species of coniferous tree in the family Podocarpaceae. The health benefits of Nageia cuspidata include its use in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever reduction, antimicrobial properties, and anti-inflammatory effects. The bioactive constituents of this plant include nagein, a lignan with potential antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, as well as other compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids. In traditional herbal medicine, Nageia cuspidata is commonly prepared as an infusion or tincture, as well as being used in the production of essential oils and other phytotherapeutic products.
